children.name: Afropavo
children.description: A monotypic African genus containing the Congo Peafowl, the only peafowl native to Africa, found in the dense lowland rainforests of the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

children.name: Alectoris
children.description: A genus of 7 rock-partridge species distributed across southern Europe, the Middle East, and Central Asia, characterized by bold facial and flank markings and a preference for rocky, arid hillsides.

children.name: Ammoperdix
children.description: A genus of 2 sand-partridge species adapted to arid and semi-arid rocky deserts from the Sinai and Middle East east to Pakistan, notable for their cryptic sandy plumage.

children.name: Arborophila
children.description: A genus of roughly 20 hill-partridge species confined to the forest understory of South and Southeast Asia, typically identified by colorful throat and breast patterns and a reluctance to fly.

children.name: Argusianus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Great Argus, a large pheasant of the lowland rainforests of Borneo and the Malay Peninsula, renowned for the male's enormously elongated secondary wing feathers covered in eye-like ocelli used in elaborate courtship displays.

children.name: Bambusicola
children.description: A genus of 3 bamboo-partridge species from South and East Asia, including the familiar Chinese Bamboo Partridge, typically found in bamboo thickets and dense scrub at forest edges.

children.name: Bonasa
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Ruffed Grouse, a forest grouse of North America's deciduous and mixed woodlands, best known for the male's drumming display produced by rapid wing-beats.

children.name: Caloperdix
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Ferruginous Partridge, a secretive, rufous-and-black-spotted forest partridge of the Thai–Malay Peninsula and Borneo.

children.name: Campocolinus
children.description: A small genus of 3 African francolins found in sub-Saharan savanna and grassland habitats, separated from the larger Francolinus complex based on molecular evidence.

children.name: Canachites
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Spruce Grouse, a tame and well-camouflaged North American grouse specialized for coniferous forest habitats, subsisting heavily on pine and spruce needles in winter.

children.name: Catreus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Cheer Pheasant, a vulnerable long-tailed pheasant of steep grassy and scrubby hillsides in the western Himalayas from Pakistan to northwestern India.

children.name: Centrocercus
children.description: A genus of 2 sage-grouse species endemic to the sagebrush steppes of western North America, the largest North American grouse, famous for spectacular lekking displays at communal grounds.

children.name: Chrysolophus
children.description: A genus of 2 spectacularly colored ruffed pheasants from the mountain forests of central China, comprising the Golden Pheasant and Lady Amherst's Pheasant, both widely kept in aviculture worldwide.

children.name: Coturnix
children.description: A genus of 4–5 true quail species distributed across Europe, Africa, and Asia, including the migratory Common Quail and the domesticated Japanese Quail widely used in research and food production.

children.name: Crossoptilon
children.description: A genus of 4 eared-pheasant species native to the high-altitude forests and scrub of central China and Tibet, recognized by their velvety plumage, red facial skin, and distinctive ear-like white cheek tufts.

children.name: Dendragapus
children.description: A genus of 2 blue-grouse species—the Dusky and Sooty Grouse—inhabiting montane coniferous forests along the Pacific coast and Rocky Mountains of western North America.

children.name: Falcipennis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Siberian Grouse, a small, dark forest grouse of the taiga zone from the Russian Far East through Siberia, closely related to the Spruce Grouse of North America.

children.name: Francolinus
children.description: A genus of 3 francolins found from sub-Saharan Africa through South and Southeast Asia, including the Black Francolin, characterized by boldly patterned plumage and loud, far-carrying calls.

children.name: Galloperdix
children.description: A genus of 3 spurfowl species endemic to the Indian subcontinent, distinguished by the males' multiple leg spurs and their preference for dense forest undergrowth.

children.name: Gallus
children.description: A genus of 4 junglefowl species native to South and Southeast Asia, including the Red Junglefowl (Gallus gallus), the wild ancestor of the domestic chicken and arguably the most economically important bird in human history.

children.name: Gallus gallus
children.description: The red junglefowl, also known as the Indian red junglefowl, is a species of tropical, galliform bird in the phasianid family, found across much of Southeast Asia and parts of South Asia.

children.name: Gallus gallus domesticus
children.description: Gallus gallus domesticus (Domestic Chicken) is a junglefowl species found in its native habitat.

children.name: Haematortyx
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crimsonhead Partridge, a little-known, blood-red-headed partridge confined to montane forest in Borneo.

children.name: Ithaginis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Blood Pheasant, a small, partridge-like pheasant of high-altitude Himalayan and Tibetan conifer and rhododendron forests, with the male's striking crimson-and-green plumage.

children.name: Lagopus
children.description: A genus of 3 ptarmigan species adapted to Arctic and alpine tundra across the Northern Hemisphere, uniquely among grouse molting into pure-white plumage each winter for camouflage in snow.

children.name: Lerwa
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Snow Partridge, a finely barred and rufous-tailed partridge of the high-altitude rocky slopes and alpine meadows of the Himalayas and Tibetan Plateau.

children.name: Lophophorus
children.description: A genus of 3 monal pheasant species inhabiting alpine forests and meadows of the Himalayas and adjacent ranges, the males being among the most brilliantly iridescent of all birds.

children.name: Lophura
children.description: A diverse genus of about 12 pheasant species found across South and Southeast Asia, including the Silver Pheasant and Kalij Pheasant, typically featuring males with iridescent blue-black plumage, red facial wattles, and elaborate tails.

children.name: Lyrurus
children.description: A genus of 2 black grouse species from Eurasia—the widespread Black Grouse and the Caucasian Grouse—known for their lyre-shaped tails and communal lek displays on open heathlands and forest edges.

children.name: Lyrurus tetrix
children.description: The black grouse, also known as northern black grouse, Eurasian black grouse, blackgame or blackcock, is a large bird in the grouse tribe. It is a sedentary species, spanning across the Palearctic in moorland and steppe habitat when breeding, often near wooded areas. They will spend the winter perched in dense forests, feeding almost exclusively on the needles of conifers.

children.name: Margaroperdix
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Madagascar Partridge, an endemic ground-dwelling partridge of Madagascar's grasslands and scrubby hillsides.

children.name: Melanoperdix
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Black Partridge, a small, nearly all-black forest partridge restricted to lowland rainforests of the Malay Peninsula and Borneo.

children.name: Meleagris
children.description: A genus of 2 turkey species native to North and Central America, including the Wild Turkey (ancestor of the domestic turkey) and the iridescent Ocellated Turkey of the Yucatán Peninsula.

children.name: Meleagris gallopavo f. domestica
children.description: Meleagris gallopavo f. domestica (Domestic Turkey) is a turkey species found in its native habitat.

children.name: Ortygornis
children.description: A genus of 3 francolins from Africa and South Asia—including the widespread Grey Francolin—inhabiting dry scrub, grassland, and agricultural areas from the Indian subcontinent to East Africa.

children.name: Pavo
children.description: A genus of 2 peafowl species from South and Southeast Asia, including the Indian Peafowl whose male bears the longest tail-train of any bird, used in elaborate fanned courtship displays.

children.name: Peliperdix
children.description: A small genus of African francolins (currently treated as 1–2 species) inhabiting forest edges and woodland in central and western Africa, recently split from the broader Francolinus assemblage.

children.name: Perdicula
children.description: A genus of 4 small bush-quail species endemic to the Indian subcontinent, inhabiting open grasslands, scrub, and dry hillsides, and distinguished by their compact size and rapid, whirring flight.

children.name: Perdix
children.description: A genus of 3 true partridge species spread across Europe and Asia, including the Grey Partridge widely hunted as a gamebird, characterized by rotund bodies, orange-buff faces, and a preference for open farmland and steppe.

children.name: Phasianus
children.description: A genus of 2 pheasant species—the Common Pheasant and the Green Pheasant—native to Asia, with the highly polytypic Common Pheasant (30 subspecies) introduced as a gamebird across much of the world.

children.name: Phasianus colchicus
children.description: The common pheasant, ring-necked pheasant, or blue-headed pheasant, is a bird in the pheasant family (Phasianidae). The genus name comes from Latin phasianus 'pheasant'. The species name colchicus is Latin for 'of Colchis', a country on the Black Sea where pheasants became known to Europeans.

children.name: Polyplectron
children.description: A genus of 8 peacock-pheasant species from Southeast Asian forests, notable for the dense iridescent ocelli covering the wings and tail that the male fans in display, analogous to but convergent with true peafowl.

children.name: Pternistis
children.description: The largest francolin genus with about 25 species distributed across sub-Saharan Africa, occupying diverse habitats from forest and scrub to open grassland, many species identified by distinctive bare facial skin patches.

children.name: Pucrasia
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Koklass Pheasant, a medium-sized, cryptically patterned pheasant of the forested Himalayan and Chinese mountain slopes, notable for its loud, distinctive call.

children.name: Rheinardia
children.description: A genus of 2 crested-argus species from the mountain forests of Vietnam, Laos, and Borneo, the males bearing extraordinarily long, wide, ocellated tail feathers that rival those of the Great Argus.

children.name: Rheinardia nigrescens
children.description: The Malayan crested argus is a large and spectacular peafowl-like species of bird in the pheasant family with dark-brown-spotted black and buff plumage, a heavy pink bill, brown irises and blue skin around the eyes. The head has two crests; the hind crest, which extends down the occiput, is erected when alarmed and during intentional behaviors including pair bonding and courtship displays. The male has a broad and greatly elongated tail of twelve feathers.

children.name: Rheinardia ocellata
children.description: The Vietnamese crested argus is a large and spectacular peafowl-like species of bird in the pheasant family with dark-brown-spotted black and buff plumage, a heavy pink bill, brown irises and blue skin around the eyes. The head has two crests; the hind crest, which extends down the occiput, is erected when alarmed and during intentional behaviors including pair bonding and courtship displays. The male has a broad and greatly elongated tail of twelve feathers.

children.name: Rhizothera
children.description: A genus of 2 long-billed partridge species from the lowland and montane forests of Borneo and the Malay Peninsula, characterized by their unusually long bills used to probe for invertebrates in leaf litter.

children.name: Rollulus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Crested Partridge (or Roul-roul), a striking forest partridge of the Thai–Malay Peninsula and Borneo, with the male's vivid green-and-red plumage and bristly red crest.

children.name: Scleroptila
children.description: A genus of about 8 African francolins found primarily in southern and eastern Africa, inhabiting highland grasslands and montane scrub, several species being characteristic birds of the highveld.

children.name: Synoicus
children.description: A genus of 4 button-quail-like quail species from Africa, South Asia, and Australasia, including the widespread Brown Quail of Australia and New Guinea, occupying grasslands and grassy woodland.

children.name: Syrmaticus
children.description: A genus of 5 long-tailed pheasants native to the mountain forests of East Asia, including Reeves's Pheasant with the longest tail of any wild bird, and several threatened island endemics.

children.name: Syrmaticus humiae
children.description: #REDIRECT Mrs.

children.name: Syrmaticus soemmerringii
children.description: #REDIRECT Copper pheasant Category:Redirects from scientific names of birds.

children.name: Tetrao
children.description: A genus of 2 capercaillie species from the boreal and mountain forests of Eurasia, including the Western Capercaillie, the largest grouse in the world and famed for its elaborate gurgling lekking song.

children.name: Tetraogallus
children.description: A genus of 5 snowcock species inhabiting the highest alpine zones of central Asia and the Caucasus—from the Himalayas to the Altai—among the world's highest-altitude birds, capable of living at elevations above 5,000 m.

children.name: Tetraophasis
children.description: A genus of 2 monal-partridge species found in the high-altitude bamboo and rhododendron forests of southwestern China and Tibet, intermediate in appearance between snowcocks and true pheasants.

children.name: Tetraophasis obscurus
children.description: #REDIRECT Chestnut-throated monal-partridge.

children.name: Tetrastes
children.description: A genus of 2 hazel-grouse species from Eurasia—the widespread Hazel Grouse and the Chinese Grouse—small, secretive forest grouse of mixed and deciduous taiga from Western Europe to East Asia.

children.name: Tragopan
children.description: A genus of 5 horned pheasant species from the Himalayan and Chinese mountain forests, the males displaying brilliantly spotted and wattled plumage and colorful inflatable facial lappets during courtship.

children.name: Tropicoperdix
children.description: A genus of 2 green-legged partridge species from the tropical forests of mainland Southeast Asia, recently separated from Arborophila based on morphological and molecular distinctions.

children.name: Tympanuchus
children.description: A genus of 3 prairie-chicken and sharp-tailed grouse species endemic to the grasslands and open country of central North America, known for spectacular group lekking displays with inflated colored air sacs.

children.name: Xenoperdix
children.description: A genus of 2 Tanzanian forest partridge species confined to the montane forests of the Eastern Arc Mountains of Tanzania, among the most recently discovered and least-known African gamebirds.
